description: Initialize displacement, velocity and position of all structure nodes in the range.

keywords.name: position
keywords.description: position of node (global system). This is used for positioning of structural elements, and must be used after creating the node, but before executing any cycles. A node that is moved into a zone will have its link deleted (if one is present) and will have a new link created with attachment conditions corresponding to the type of structural element using the node (see this table ). If more than one element type is using the node, then the attachment condition will correspond with the first element type in the following list: liner, geogrid, pile, cable, shell, and beam. Thus, if a node is being used by both a geogrid and a cable, then the attachment condition after moving this node will correspond with that of a geogrid.

keywords.name: position-x
keywords.description: \(x\) -coordinate of node (global system). See position above.

keywords.name: position-y
keywords.description: \(y\) -coordinate of node (global system). See position above.

keywords.name: position-z
keywords.description: \(z\) -coordinate of node (global system). See position above.

keywords.name: ratio-target
keywords.description: Sets the target local force ratio that is considered to be converged for mechanical calculations. The default value is 1e-4. A local force ratio of this value will result in a convergence value of 1.0 for the node. See the convergence keyword in the model solve command.

keywords.name: add
keywords.description: Add the specified value to the existing value.

keywords.name: gradient
keywords.description: Apply a gradient to the scalar-value provided.

keywords.name: local
keywords.description: Specify that the value supplied for the keyword is in the local coordinate system of the gridpoint (if any). By default values are initialize in the global system.

keywords.name: multiply
keywords.description: Multiply the existing value by the specified value.

keywords.name: vary
keywords.description: Apply a linear variation to the scalar-value provided.
